sections.title: Critical
sections.content: Comprehensive protocol for accidental opioid overdose response including NARCAN (naloxone) administration and recovery positioning. Designed for professional medical first responders, EMTs, and law enforcement personnel.

sections.title: Warning
sections.content: NARCAN effects are temporary. Patient may re-overdose when it wears off!

steps.title: Scene Safety Assessment
steps.subtitle: Ensure scene is safe. Check for hazards, drug paraphernalia, or hostile individuals. Use universal precautions.

steps.title: Assess Responsiveness
steps.subtitle: Tap shoulder firmly and shout. Check for response using sternal rub if needed. Look for signs of opioid overdose.

steps.title: Call Emergency Services
steps.subtitle: Call 911 immediately. Report suspected opioid overdose and request Advance Life Support if available. Provide exact location.

steps.title: Assess Breathing & Pulse
steps.subtitle: Check for adequate breathing (rate >10 breaths/minute, good depth). Assess pulse, skin color, look for cyanosis (bluish or purplish discoloration). Check Pupils (pin-point pupils sign of opiate overdose).

steps.title: Administer NARCAN (Naloxone)
steps.subtitle: If available, give intranasal or 0.4–2mg IV/IM. May repeat every 2–3 minutes if no response. Max 3 doses.

steps.title: Airway Management
steps.subtitle: Open airway using head-tilt, chin-lift. Clear visible Obstructions.

steps.title: Rescue Breathing/Ventilation
steps.subtitle: If breathing inadequate: provide rescue breaths with Bag-Valve-Mask or pocket mask. Rate 10–12/min for adults.

steps.title: Recovery Position
steps.subtitle: If breathing adequately, place in recovery position on their side, top leg bent to prevent rolling, and to prevent aspiration if vomiting occurs.

steps.title: CPR if Cardiac Arrest
steps.subtitle: If no pulse, begin CPR immediately. 30:2 compression to ventilation ratio. Continue until Advanced Life Support arrives.

steps.title: Monitor Patient Response
steps.subtitle: Watch for improvement after NARCAN. Be prepared for agitation or combativeness. Monitor vital signs.
